_attribution: LA FORJA team (Manuel Peña Zúñiga). Original item authored by the team.
stem: Two fair six-sided dice are rolled. What is the probability of getting a 6?
author_rationale: The author had 'at least one 6' in mind: 36 outcomes, 11 favorable, P = 11/36.

intended_defect.description: The phrase 'getting a 6' admits at least two reasonable readings: 'at least one 6' and 'exactly one 6'. The stem does not disambiguate.
intended_defect.expected_finding: Reading A = 'at least one 6' → 11/36. Reading B = 'exactly one 6' → 10/36. The answers differ, so the ambiguity attack is valid.
